name: orchestrator-workers
summary: Lead agent decomposes the task into subtasks at runtime and spawns workers. Unlike prompt chaining, subtasks emerge during work — not pre-specified.
when_to_use: Large features where the subtask shape can't be known in advance. E.g. 'migrate every API endpoint to the new schema' — the orchestrator discovers endpoints, then spawns workers.

layers.hook: Optional: idle-enforcement hook to clean up worker processes.
layers.agent: agents/team-orchestrator is the lead. Spawns role-based workers (implementer, debugger, etc.) as needed. pattern-router may pre-select this pattern.

cost: ~2–5× normal. Orchestrator overhead + N workers. Budget cap via /cc-orchestrate --budget.
anti_patterns: Orchestrator-workers for fixed-scope work — supervisor overhead dominates.
Unbounded worker spawning — can balloon cost.
No structured handoff between orchestrator and workers — workers drift off-scope.
